fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><h3 id="arguments" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Arguments<a class="headerlink" href="#arguments" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><ul><li><code>value</code>: A <code>DateTime64</code> value with any precision. <a href="../data-types/datetime64">DateTime64</a>.</li></ul><h3 id="returns" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Returns<a class="headerlink" href="#returns" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><p>An <code>Int64</code> Unix timestamp representing nanoseconds since the Unix Epoch in UTC. <a href="../data-types/int-uint">Int64</a>.</p><h3 id="example" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Example<a class="headerlink" href="#example" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">WITH toDateTime64(&#x27;1970-01-01 00:20:34.567891011&#x27;, 9, &#x27;UTC&#x27;) AS dt64
SELECT toUnixTimestamp64Nano(dt64)
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><p>Result:</p><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">┌─toUnixTimestamp64Nano(dt64)─┐
│               1234567891011 │
└─────────────────────────────┘
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><h2 id="fromunixtimestamp64second" class="t-h4 font-semibold mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="12.0">fromUnixTimestamp64Second<a class="headerlink" href="#fromunixtimestamp64second" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h2><p>Converts an <code>Int64</code> Unix timestamp (seconds) to a <code>DateTime64</code> value with second precision, optionally specifying a timezone.</p><h3 id="syntax" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Syntax<a class="headerlink" href="#syntax" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">fromUnixTimestamp64Second(value[, timezone])
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><h3 id="arguments" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Arguments<a class="headerlink" href="#arguments" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><ul><li><code>value</code>: An <code>Int64</code> Unix timestamp representing seconds since the Unix Epoch in UTC. <a href="../data-types/int-uint">Int64</a>.</li><li><code>timezone</code> (optional): A string specifying the timezone for the resulting <code>DateTime64</code> value. <a href="../data-types/string">String</a>.</li></ul><h3 id="returns" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Returns<a class="headerlink" href="#returns" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><p>A <code>DateTime64</code> value with 0 precision (seconds). <a href="../data-types/datetime64">DateTime64</a>.</p><h3 id="example" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Example<a class="headerlink" href="#example" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">WITH CAST(1733935988, &#x27;Int64&#x27;) AS i64
SELECT
    fromUnixTimestamp64Second(i64, &#x27;UTC&#x27;) AS x,
    toTypeName(x)
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><p>Result:</p><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">┌───────────────────x─┬─toTypeName(x)────────┐
│ 2024-12-11 16:53:08 │ DateTime64(0, &#x27;UTC&#x27;) │
└─────────────────────┴──────────────────────┘
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><h2 id="fromunixtimestamp64milli" class="t-h4 font-semibold mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="12.0">fromUnixTimestamp64Milli<a class="headerlink" href="#fromunixtimestamp64milli" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h2><p>Converts an <code>Int64</code> Unix timestamp (milliseconds) to a <code>DateTime64</code> value with millisecond precision, optionally specifying a timezone.</p><h3 id="syntax" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Syntax<a class="headerlink" href="#syntax" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">fromUnixTimestamp64Milli(value[, timezone])
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><h3 id="arguments" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Arguments<a class="headerlink" href="#arguments" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><ul><li><code>value</code>: An <code>Int64</code> Unix timestamp representing milliseconds since the Unix Epoch in UTC. <a href="../data-types/int-uint">Int64</a>.</li><li><code>timezone</code> (optional): A string specifying the timezone for the resulting <code>DateTime64</code> value. <a href="../data-types/string">String</a>.</li></ul><h3 id="returns" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Returns<a class="headerlink" href="#returns" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><p>A <code>DateTime64</code> value with 3 precision (milliseconds). <a href="../data-types/datetime64">DateTime64</a>.</p><h3 id="example" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Example<a class="headerlink" href="#example" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">WITH CAST(1733935988123, &#x27;Int64&#x27;) AS i64
SELECT
    fromUnixTimestamp64Milli(i64, &#x27;UTC&#x27;) AS x,
    toTypeName(x)
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><p>Result:</p><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">┌───────────────────────x─┬─toTypeName(x)────────┐
│ 2024-12-11 16:53:08.123 │ DateTime64(3, &#x27;UTC&#x27;) │
└─────────────────────────┴──────────────────────┘
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><h2 id="fromunixtimestamp64micro" class="t-h4 font-semibold mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="12.0">fromUnixTimestamp64Micro<a class="headerlink" href="#fromunixtimestamp64micro" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h2><p>Converts an <code>Int64</code> Unix timestamp (microseconds) to a <code>DateTime64</code> value with microsecond precision, optionally specifying a timezone.</p><h3 id="syntax" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Syntax<a class="headerlink" href="#syntax" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">fromUnixTimestamp64Micro(value[, timezone])
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><h3 id="arguments" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Arguments<a class="headerlink" href="#arguments" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><ul><li><code>value</code>: An <code>Int64</code> Unix timestamp representing microseconds since the Unix Epoch in UTC. <a href="../data-types/int-uint">Int64</a>.</li><li><code>timezone</code> (optional): A string specifying the timezone for the resulting <code>DateTime64</code> value. <a href="../data-types/string">String</a>.</li></ul><h3 id="returns" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Returns<a class="headerlink" href="#returns" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><p>A <code>DateTime64</code> value with 6 precision (microseconds). <a href="../data-types/datetime64">DateTime64</a>.</p><h3 id="example" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Example<a class="headerlink" href="#example" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">WITH CAST(1733935988123456, &#x27;Int64&#x27;) AS i64
SELECT
    fromUnixTimestamp64Micro(i64, &#x27;UTC&#x27;) AS x,
    toTypeName(x)
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><p>Result:</p><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">┌──────────────────────────x─┬─toTypeName(x)────────┐
│ 2024-12-11 16:53:08.123456 │ DateTime64(6, &#x27;UTC&#x27;) │
└────────────────────────────┴──────────────────────┘
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><h2 id="fromunixtimestamp64nano" class="t-h4 font-semibold mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="12.0">fromUnixTimestamp64Nano<a class="headerlink" href="#fromunixtimestamp64nano" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h2><p>Converts an <code>Int64</code> Unix timestamp (nanoseconds) to a <code>DateTime64</code> value with nanosecond precision, optionally specifying a timezone.</p><h3 id="syntax" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Syntax<a class="headerlink" href="#syntax" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">fromUnixTimestamp64Nano(value[, timezone])
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><h3 id="arguments" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Arguments<a class="headerlink" href="#arguments" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><ul><li><code>value</code>: An <code>Int64</code> Unix timestamp representing nanoseconds since the Unix Epoch in UTC. <a href="../data-types/int-uint">Int64</a>.</li><li><code>timezone</code> (optional): A string specifying the timezone for the resulting <code>DateTime64</code> value. <a href="../data-types/string">String</a>.</li></ul><h3 id="returns" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Returns<a class="headerlink" href="#returns" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><p>A <code>DateTime64</code> value with 9 precision (nanoseconds). <a href="../data-types/datetime64">DateTime64</a>.</p><h3 id="example" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Example<a class="headerlink" href="#example" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">WITH CAST(1733935988123456789, &#x27;Int64&#x27;) AS i64
SELECT
    fromUnixTimestamp64Nano(i64, &#x27;UTC&#x27;) AS x,
    toTypeName(x)
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><p>Result:</p><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">┌─────────────────────────────x─┬─toTypeName(x)────────┐
│ 2024-12-11 16:53:08.123456789 │ DateTime64(9, &#x27;UTC&#x27;) │
└───────────────────────────────┴──────────────────────┘
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><h2 id="formatrow" class="t-h4 font-semibold mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="12.0">formatRow<a class="headerlink" href="#formatrow" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h2><p>Formats multiple expressions into a single string using a specified text format. The output typically includes a newline character at the end.</p><h3 id="syntax" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Syntax<a class="headerlink" href="#syntax" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">formatRow(format, x, y, ...)
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><h3 id="arguments" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Arguments<a class="headerlink" href="#arguments" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><ul><li><code>format</code>: The name of the text format to use (e.g., <code>&#x27;CSV&#x27;</code>, <code>&#x27;TSV&#x27;</code>).</li><li><code>x</code>, <code>y</code>, ...: The expressions to format.</li></ul><h3 id="returns" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Returns<a class="headerlink" href="#returns" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><p>A formatted string, usually terminated with a newline character. <a href="../data-types/string">String</a>.</p><h3 id="example" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Example<a class="headerlink" href="#example" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">SELECT formatRow(&#x27;CSV&#x27;, number, &#x27;good&#x27;)
FROM numbers(3)
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><p>Result:</p><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">┌─formatRow(&#x27;CSV&#x27;, number, &#x27;good&#x27;)─┐
│ 0,&quot;good&quot;
                         │
│ 1,&quot;good&quot;
                         │
│ 2,&quot;good&quot;
                         │
└──────────────────────────────────┘
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><h2 id="formatrownonewline" class="t-h4 font-semibold mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="12.0">formatRowNoNewline<a class="headerlink" href="#formatrownonewline" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h2><p>Formats multiple expressions into a single string using a specified text format, similar to <code>formatRow</code>, but removes any trailing newline character.</p><h3 id="syntax" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Syntax<a class="headerlink" href="#syntax" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">formatRowNoNewline(format, x, y, ...)
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><h3 id="arguments" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Arguments<a class="headerlink" href="#arguments" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><ul><li><code>format</code>: The name of the text format to use (e.g., <code>&#x27;CSV&#x27;</code>, <code>&#x27;TSV&#x27;</code>).</li><li><code>x</code>, <code>y</code>, ...: The expressions to format.</li></ul><h3 id="returns" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Returns<a class="headerlink" href="#returns" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><p>A formatted string, without a trailing newline character. <a href="../data-types/string">String</a>.</p><h3 id="example" class="t-body-1 font-medium mt-10 mb-2" data-pagefind-weight="8.0">Example<a class="headerlink" href="#example" title="Permalink to this heading" data-pagefind-ignore="true">¶</a></h3><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">SELECT formatRowNoNewline(&#x27;CSV&#x27;, number, &#x27;good&#x27;)
FROM numbers(3)
</pre></div></div><div class="absolute top-0 right-0 p-2"><button class="snippet-copyButton"><svg class="" width="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><g opacity="1"><mask id="w-mask" fill="white"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M13 3H7V9H13V3ZM7 2C6.44772 2 6 2.44772 6 3V9C6 9.55228 6.44772 10 7 10H13C13.5523 10 14 9.55228 14 9V3C14 2.44772 13.5523 2 13 2H7Z"></path></mask><path mask="url(#w-mask)" fill="#1FCC83" d="M7 3V2H6V3H7ZM13 3H14V2H13V3ZM7 9H6V10H7V9ZM13 9V10H14V9H13ZM7 4H13V2H7V4ZM8 9V3H6V9H8ZM13 8H7V10H13V8ZM12 3V9H14V3H12ZM7 3V1C5.89543 1 5 1.89543 5 3H7ZM7 9V3H5V9H7ZM7 9H5C5 10.1046 5.89543 11 7 11V9ZM13 9H7V11H13V9ZM13 9V11C14.1046 11 15 10.1046 15 9H13ZM13 3V9H15V3H13ZM13 3H15C15 1.89543 14.1046 1 13 1V3ZM7 3H13V1H7V3Z"></path><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M6 7H3V13H9V10H10V13C10 13.5523 9.55228 14 9 14H3C2.44772 14 2 13.5523 2 13V7C2 6.44772 2.44772 6 3 6H6V7Z" fill="#1FCC83"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div><p>Result:</p><div class="max-w-full overflow-auto mb-4"><div class="snippet"><div class="snippet-code-container"><div class="snippet-code is-visible"><pre class="cm-s-default">┌─formatRowNoNewline(&#x27;CSV&#x27;, number, &#x27;good&#x27;)─┐

94-6ee008eb8be89699.js\",\"9267\",\"static/chunks/9267-89ff5dc708370f39.js\",\"1284\",\"static/chunks/1284-f30446673b9ba073.js\",\"7867\",\"static/chunks/7867-6d0484accd87a171.js\",\"3145\",\"static/chunks/3145-ca0c40eb1bb881d8.js\",\"5107\",\"static/chunks/5107-ef688f2fbbe55825.js\",\"2418\",\"static/chunks/2418-b0d27e6f47546ed6.js\",\"4144\",\"static/chunks/4144-34f98e6e70550e12.js\",\"1147\",\"static/chunks/1147-ac53c80b8f8a3921.js\",\"1749\",\"static/chunks/1749-b2df567f1b8974df.js\",\"564"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"3\",\"static/chunks/5643-5ba78075e5c8bae2.js\",\"1796\",\"static/chunks/1796-b956fe6fe21dbf17.js\",\"787\",\"static/chunks/787-09fc1907ef45c351.js\",\"996\",\"static/chunks/996-ce0ceee74ea8f43a.js\",\"7432\",\"static/chunks/7432-03643de0998568d4.js\",\"5077\",\"static/chunks/app/(sql-reference)/sql-reference/%5B%5B...slug%5D%5D/page-1cd32edffe376f31.js\"],\"default\"]\n25:I[72972,[\"5878\",\"static/chunks/9da6db1e-fe3f3691e817470b.js\",\"314\",\"static/chunks/32ea55aa-334b9848c750da09.js\",\"4960\",\"static/chunks/8b30ef62-8e1a3557163ef34d.js\",\"9729\",\"static/chunks/72b2f85d-e46ed6c2f9c8d9d7.js\",\"5501\",\"static/chunks/c16f53c3-0400c7825a916970.js\",\"8370\",\"static/chunks/8370-7a9554878d1b9d35.js\",\"7648\",\"static/chunks/7648-2c0c3a3dc3702719.js\",\"2694\",\"static/chunks/2694-6ee008eb8be89699.js\",\"9267\",\"static/chunks/9267-89ff5dc708370f39.js\",\"1284\",\"static/chunks/1284-f30446673b9ba073.js\",\"7867\",\"static/chunks/7867-6d0484accd87a171.js\",\"3145\",\"static/chunks/3145-ca0c40eb1bb881d8.js\",\"5107\",\"static/chunks/5107-ef688f2fbbe55825.js\",\"2418\",\"static/chunks/2418-b0d27e6f47546ed6.js\",\"4144\",\"static/chunks/4144-34f98e6e70550e12.js\",\"1147\",\"static/chunks/1147-ac53c80b8f8a3921.js\",\"1749\",\"static/chunks/1749-b2df567f1b8974df.js\",\"5643\",\"static/chunks/5643-5ba78075e5c8bae2.js\",\"1796\",\"static/chunks/1796-b956fe6fe21dbf17.js\",\"787\",\"static/chunks/787-09fc1907ef45c351.js\",\"996\",\"static/c "static/chunks/7432-03643de0998568d4.js\",\"5077\",\"static/chunks/app/(sql-reference)/sql-reference/%5B%5B...slug%5D%5D/page-1cd32edffe376f31.js\"],\"default\"]\n227:I[14543,[\"5878\",\"static/chunks/9da6db1e-fe3f3691e817470b.js\",\"314\",\"static/chunks/32ea55aa-334b9848c750da09.js\",\"4960\",\"static/chunks/8b30ef62-8e1a3557163ef34d.js\",\"9729\",\"static/chunks/72b2f85d-e46ed6c2f9c8d9d7.js\",\"5501\",\"static/chunks/c16f53c3-0400c7825a916970.js\",\"8370\",\"static/chunks/8370-7a9554878d1b9d35.js\",\"7648\",\"static/chunks/7648-2c0c3a3dc3702719.js\",\"2694\",\"static/chunks/2694-6ee008eb8be89699.js\",\"9267\",\"static/chunks/9267-89ff5dc708370f39.js\",\"1284\",\"static/chunks/1284-f30446673b9ba073.js\",\"7867\",\"static/chunks/7867-6d0484accd87a171.js\",\"3145\",\"static/chunks/3145-ca0c40eb1bb881d8.js\",\"5107\",\"static/chunks/5107-ef688f2fbbe55825.js\",\"2418\",\"static/chunks/2418-b0d27e6f47546ed6.js\",\"4144\",\"static/chunks/4144-34f98e6e70550e12.js\",\"1147\",\"static/chunks/1147-ac53c80b8f8a3921.js\",\"1749\",\"static/chunks/1749-b2df567f1b8974df.js\",\"5643\",\"static/chunks/5643-5ba78075e5c8bae2.js\",\"1796\",\"static/chunks/1796-b956fe6fe21dbf17.js\",\"787\",\"static/chunks/787-09fc1907ef45c351.js\",\"996\",\"static/chunks/996"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"-ce0ceee74ea8f43a.js\",\"7432\",\"static/chunks/7432-03643de0998568d4.js\",\"5077\",\"static/chunks/app/(sql-reference)/sql-reference/%5B%5B...slug%5D%5D/page-1cd32edffe376f31.js\"],\"default\"]\n228:I[57187,[\"5878\",\"static/chunks/9da6db1e-fe3f3691e817470b.js\",\"314\",\"static/chunks/32ea55aa-334b9848c750da09.js\",\"4960\",\"static/chunks/8b30ef62-8e1a3557163ef34d.js\",\"9729\",\"static/chunks/72b2f85d-e46ed6c2f9c8d9d7.js\",\"5501\",\"static/chunks/c16f53c3-0400c7825a916970.js\",\"8370\",\"static/chunks/8370-7a9554878d1b9d35.js\",\"7648\",\"static/chunks/7648-2c0c3a3dc3702719.js\",\"2694\",\"static/chunks/2694-6ee008eb8be89699.js\",\"9267\",\"static/chunks/9267-89ff5dc708370f39.js\",\"1284\",\"static/chunks/1284-f30446673b9ba073.js\",\"7867\",\"static/chunks/7867-6d0484accd87a171.js\",\"3145\",\"static/chunks/3145-ca0c40eb1bb881d8.js\",\"5107\",\"static/chunks/5107-ef688f2fbbe55825.js\",\"2418\",\"static/chunks/2418-b0d27e6f47546ed6.js\",\"4144\",\"static/chunks/4144-34f98e6e70550e12.js\",\"1147\",\"static/chunks/1147-ac53c80b8f8a3921.js\",\"1749\",\"static/chunks/1749-b2df567f1b8974df.js\",\"5643\",\"static/chunks/5643-5ba78075e5c8bae2.js\",\"1796\",\"static/chunks/1796-b956fe6fe21dbf17.js\",\"787\",\"static/chunks/787-09fc1907ef45c351.js\",\"996\",\"static/chunks/996-ce0ceee74ea8f43a.js\",\"7432\",\"static/chunks/7432-03643de0998568d4.js\",\"5077\",\"static/chunks/app/(sql-reference)/sql-reference/%5B%5B...slug%5D%5D/page-1cd32edffe376f31.js\"],\"default\"]\n21:T2ebe3,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"---\ntitle: Type Conversion Functions reference\nmeta:\n    description: Functions for converting values between different data types.\nheadingMaxLevels: 2\n---\n\n# Type conversion functions\n\nThe following functions are available for type conversion.\n\n## Common issues with data conversion\n\n`to\u003ctype\u003e` functions and cast behave differently in some cases, for example in case of LowCardinality: cast removes LowCardinality trait `to\u003ctype\u003e` functions don't. The same with Nullable, this behaviour isn't compatible with SQL standard, and it can be changed using cast_keep_nullable setting.\n\n{% callout type=\"info\" %}\nBe aware of potential data loss if values of a datatype are converted to a smaller datatype (for example from `Int64` to `Int32`) or between\nincompatible datatypes (for example from `String` to `Int`). Make sure to check carefully if the result is as expected.\n{% /callout %}\n\nExample:\n\n```sql\nSELECT\n    toTypeName(toLowCardinality('') AS val) AS source_type,\n    toTypeName(toString(val)) AS to_type_result_type,\n    toTypeName(CAST(val, 'String')) AS cast_result_type\n\n┌─source_type────────────┬─to_type_result_type────┬─cast_result_type─┐\n│ LowCardinality(String) │ LowCardinality(String) │ String           │\n└────────────────────────┴────────────────────────┴──────────────────┘\n\nSELECT\n    toTypeName(toNullable('') AS val) AS source_type,\n    toTypeName(toString(val)) AS to_type_result_type,\n    toTypeName(CAST(val, 'String')) AS cast_result_type\n\n┌─source_type──────┬─to_type_result_type─┬─cast_result_type─┐\n│ Nullable(String) │ Nullable(String)    │ String           │\n└──────────────────┴─────────────────────┴──────────────────┘\n\nSELECT\n    toTypeName(toNullable('') AS val) AS source_type,\n    toTypeName(toString(val)) AS to_type_result_type,\n    toTypeName(CAST(val, 'String')) AS cast_result_type\nSETTINGS cast_keep_nullable = 1\n\n┌─source_type──────┬─to_type_result_type─┬─cast_result_type─┐\n│ Nullable(String) │ Nullable(String)    │ Nullable(String) │\n└──────────────────┴─────────────────────┴──────────────────┘\n```\n\n## toBool\n\nConverts an input value to a boolean type. This function will throw an error if the conversion is not possible.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoBool(expr)\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `expr`: An expression that evaluates to a number or a string.\n    - Supported numeric types: `Int8`, `UInt8`, `Int16`, `UInt16`, `Int32`, `UInt32`, `Int64`, `UInt64`, `Int128`, `UInt128`, `Int256`, `UInt256`, `Float32`, `Float64`.\n    - Supported string values: `'true'` or `'false'` (case-insensitive).\n\n### Returns\n\nA boolean value (`true` or `false`). [Bool](../data-types/boolean).\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT\n    toBool(toUInt8(1)),\n    toBool(toInt8(-1)),\n    toBool(toFloat32(1.01)),\n    toBool('true'),\n    toBool('false'),\n    toBool('FALSE')\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\ntoBool(toUInt8(1)):      true\ntoBool(toInt8(-1)):      true\ntoBool(toFloat32(1.01)): true\ntoBool('true'):          true\ntoBool('false'):         false\ntoBool('FALSE'):         false\n```\n\n## toInt8\n\nConverts an input value to an 8-bit signed integer. This function will throw an error if the conversion is not possible.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoInt8(expr)\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `expr`: An expression that evaluates to a number or a string representation of a number.\n    - Supported numeric types: `Int8`, `UInt8`, `Int16`, `UInt16`, `Int32`, `UInt32`, `Int64`, `UInt64`, `Int128`, `UInt128`, `Int256`, `UInt256`, `Float32`, `Float64`.\n    - Supported string representations: Integers.\n    - Unsupported string representations: Floating-point numbers (e.g., `'1.23'`), `NaN`, `Inf`, binary, or hexadecimal values.\n\n{% callout type=\"info\" %}\nIf the input value can't be represented within the bounds of Int8, overflow or underflow of the result occurs.\nThis isn't considered an error.\nFor example: `SELECT toInt8(128) == -128;`.\n{% /callout %}\n\n### Returns\n\nAn 8-bit signed integer. [Int8](../data-types/int-uint).\n\n{% callout type=\"info\" %}\nThe function uses rounding towards zero, meaning it truncates fractional digits of numbers.\n{% /callout %}\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT\n    toInt8(-8),\n    toInt8(-8.8),\n    toInt8('-8')\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\nRow 1:\n──────\ntoInt8(-8):   -8\ntoInt8(-8.8): -8\ntoInt8('-8'): -8\n```\n\n## toInt8OrZero\n\nConverts an input value to an 8-bit signed integer, returning `0` if the conversion fails.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoInt8OrZero(x)\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `x`: A string representation of a number.\n    - Supported string representations: Integers.\n    - Unsupported string representations (return `0`): Floating-point numbers (e.g., `'1.23'`), `NaN`, `Inf`, binary, or hexadecimal values.\n\n{% callout type=\"info\" %}\nIf the input value can't be represented within the bounds of Int8, overflow or underflow of the result occurs.\nThis isn't considered an error.\n{% /callout %}\n\n### Returns\n\nAn 8-bit signed integer if successful, otherwise `0`. [Int8](../data-types/int-uint).\n\n{% callout type=\"info\" %}\nThe function uses rounding towards zero, meaning it truncates fractional digits of numbers.\n{% /callout %}\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT\n    toInt8OrZero('-8'),\n    toInt8OrZero('abc')\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\nRow 1:\n──────\ntoInt8OrZero('-8'):  -8\ntoInt8OrZero('abc'): 0\n```\n\n## toInt8OrNull\n\nConverts an input value to an 8-bit signed integer, returning `NULL` if the conversion fails.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoInt8OrNull(x)\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `x`: A string representation of a number.\n    - Supported string representations: Integers.\n    - Unsupported string representations (return `NULL`): Floating-point numbers (e.g., `'1.23'`), `NaN`, `Inf`, binary, or hexadecimal values.\n\n{% callout type=\"info\" %}\nIf the input value can't be represented within the bounds of Int8, overflow or underflow of the result occurs.\nThis isn't considered an error.\n{% /callout %}\n\n### Returns\n\nAn 8-bit signed integer if successful, otherwise `NULL`. [Int8](../data-types/int-uint) / [Nullable](../data-types/nullable).\n\n{% callout type=\"info\" %}\nThe function uses rounding towards zero, meaning it truncates fractional digits of numbers.\n{% /callout %}\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT\n    toInt8OrNull('-8'),\n    toInt8OrNull('abc')\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\nRow 1:\n──────\ntoInt8OrNull('-8'):  -8\ntoInt8OrNull('abc'): ᴺᵁᴸᴸ\n```\n\n## toInt8OrDefault\n\nConverts an input value to an 8-bit signed integer, returning a specified default value if the conversion fails. If no default is provided, it returns `0`.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoInt8OrDefault(expr[, default])\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `expr`: An expression that evaluates to a number or a string representation of a number.\n    - Supported numeric types: `Int8`, `UInt8`, `Int16`, `UInt16`, `Int32`, `UInt32`, `Int64`, `UInt64`, `Int128`, `UInt128`, `Int256`, `UInt256`, `Float32`, `Float64`.\n    - Supported string representations: Integers.\n    - Unsupported string representations (return `default` or `0`): Floating-point numbers (e.g., `'1.23'`), `NaN`, `Inf`, binary, or hexadecimal values.\n- `default` (optional): The `Int8` value to return if the conversion of `expr` is unsuccessful.\n\n{% callout type=\"info\" %}\nIf the input value can't be represented within the bounds of Int8, overflow or underflow of the result occurs.\nThis isn't considered an error.\n{% /callout %}\n\n### Returns\n\nAn 8-bit signed integer if successful, otherwise the `default` value (if provided) or `0`. [Int8](../data-types/int-uint).\n\n{% callout type=\"info\" %}\n- The function uses rounding towards zero, meaning it truncates fractional digits of numbers.\n- The default value type should be the same as the cast type.\n{% /callout %}\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT\n    toInt8OrDefault('-8', CAST('-1', 'Int8')),\n    toInt8OrDefault('abc', CAST('-1', 'Int8'))\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\nRow 1:\n──────\ntoInt8OrDefault('-8', CAST('-1', 'Int8')):  -8\ntoInt8OrDefault('abc', CAST('-1', 'Int8')): -1\n```\n\n## toInt16\n\nConverts an input value to a 16-bit signed integer. This function will throw an error if the conversion is not possible.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoInt16(expr)\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `expr`: An expression that evaluates to a number or a string representation of a number.\n    - Supported numeric types: `Int8`, `UInt8`, `Int16`, `UInt16`, `Int32`, `UInt32`, `Int64`, `UInt64`, `Int128`, `UInt128`, `Int256`, `UInt256`, `Float32`, `Float64`.\n    - Supported string representations: Integers.\n    - Unsupported string representations: Floating-point numbers (e.g., `'1.23'`), `NaN`, `Inf`, binary, or hexadecimal values.\n\n{% callout type=\"info\" %}\nIf the input value can't be represented within the bounds of Int16, overflow or underflow of the result occurs.\nThis isn't considered an error.\nFor example: `SELECT toInt16(32768) == -32768;`.\n{% /callout %}\n\n### Returns\n\nA 16-bit signed integer. [Int16](../data-types/int-uint).\n\n{% callout type=\"info\" %}\nThe function uses rounding towards zero, meaning it truncates fractional digits of numbers.\n{% /callout %}\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT\n    toInt16(-16),\n    toInt16(-16.16),\n    toInt16('-16')\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\nRow 1:\n──────\ntoInt16(-16):    -16\ntoInt16(-16.16): -16\ntoInt16('-16'):  -16\n```\n\n## toInt16OrZero\n\nConverts an input value to a 16-bit signed integer, returning `0` if the conversion fails.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoInt16OrZero(x)\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `x`: A string representation of a number.\n    - Supported string representations: Integers.\n    - Unsupported string representations (return `0`): Floating-point numbers (e.g., `'1.23'`), `NaN`, `Inf`, binary, or hexadecimal values.\n\n{% callout type=\"info\" %}\nIf the input value can't be represented within the bounds of Int16, overflow or underflow of the result occurs.\nThis isn't considered as an error.\n{% /callout %}\n\n### Returns\n\nA 16-bit signed integer if successful, otherwise `0`. [Int16](../data-types/int-uint).\n\n{% callout type=\"info\" %}\nThe function uses rounding towards zero, meaning it truncates fractional digits of numbers.\n{% /callout %}\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT\n    toInt16OrZero('-16'),\n    toInt16OrZero('abc')\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\nRow 1:\n──────\ntoInt16OrZero('-16'): -16\ntoInt16OrZero('abc'): 0\n```\n\n## toInt16OrNull\n\nConverts an input value to a 16-bit signed integer, returning `NULL` if the conversion fails.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoInt16OrNull(x)\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `x`: A string representation of a number.\n    - Supported string representations: Integers.\n    - Unsupported string representations (return `NULL`): Floating-point numbers (e.g., `'1.23'`), `NaN`, `Inf`, binary, or hexadecimal values.\n\n{% callout type=\"info\" %}\nIf the input value can't be represented within the bounds of Int16, overflow or underflow of the result occurs.\nThis isn't considered an error.\n{% /callout %}\n\n### Returns\n\nA 16-bit signed integer if successful, otherwise `NULL`. [Int16](../data-types/int-uint) / [Nullable](../data-types/nullable).\n\n{% callout type=\"info\" %}\nThe function uses rounding towards zero, meaning it truncates fractional digits of numbers.\n{% /callout %}\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT\n    toInt16OrNull('-16'),\n    toInt16OrNull('abc')\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\nRow 1:\n──────\ntoInt16OrNull('-16'): -16\ntoInt16OrNull('abc'): ᴺᵁᴸᴸ\n```\n\n## toInt16OrDefault\n\nConverts an input value to a 16-bit signed integer, returning a specified default value if the conversion fails. If no default is provided, it returns `0`.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoInt16OrDefault(expr[, default])\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `expr`: An expression that evaluates to a number or a string representation of a number.\n    - Supported numeric types: `Int8`, `UInt8`, `Int16`, `UInt16`, `Int32`, `UInt32`, `Int64`, `UInt64`, `Int128`, `UInt128`, `Int256`, `UInt256`, `Float32`, `Float64`.\n    - Supported string representations: Integers.\n    - Unsupported string representations (return `default` or `0`): Floating-point numbers (e.g., `'1.23'`), `NaN`, `Inf`, binary, or hexadecimal values.\n- `default` (optional): The `Int16` value to return if the conversion of `expr` is unsuccessful.\n\n{% callout type=\"info\" %}\nIf the input value can't be represented within the bounds of Int16, overflow or underflow of the result occurs.\nThis isn't considered an error.\n{% /callout %}\n\n### Returns\n\nA 16-bit signed integer if successful, otherwise the `default` value (if provided) or `0`. [Int16](../data-types/int-uint).\n\n{% callout type=\"info\" %}\n- The function uses rounding towards zero, meaning it truncates fractional digits of numbers.\n- The default value type should be the same as the cast type.\n{% /callout %}\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT\n    toInt16OrDefault('-16', CAST('-1', 'Int16')),\n    toInt16OrDefault('abc', CAST('-1', 'Int16'))\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\nRow 1:\n──────\ntoInt16OrDefault('-16', CAST('-1', 'Int16')): -16\ntoInt16OrDefault('abc', CAST('-1', 'Int16')): -1\n```\n\n## toInt32\n\nConverts an input value to a 32-bit signed integer. This function will throw an error if the conversion is not possible.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoInt32(expr)\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `expr`: An expression that evaluates to a number or a string representation of a number.\n    - Supported numeric types: `Int8`, `UInt8`, `Int16`, `UInt16`, `Int32`, `UInt32`, `Int64`, `UInt64`, `Int128`, `UInt128`, `Int256`, `UInt256`, `Float32`, `Float64`.\n    - Supported string representations: Integers.\n    - Unsupported string representations: Floating-point numbers (e.g., `'1.23'`), `NaN`, `Inf`, binary, or hexadecimal values.\n\n{% callout type=\"info\" %}\nIf the input value can't be represented within the bounds of Int32, the result over or under flows.\nThis isn't considered an error.\nFor example: `SELECT toInt32(2147483648) == -2147483648;`\n{% /callout %}\n\n### Returns\n\nA 32-bit signed integer. If no default is provided, it returns `0`.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoInt32OrDefault(expr[, default])\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `expr`: An expression that evaluates to a number or a string representation of a number.\n    - Supported numeric types: `Int8`, `UInt8`, `Int16`, `UInt16`, `Int32`, `UInt32`, `Int64`, `UInt64`, `Int128`, `UInt128`, `Int256`, `UInt256`, `Float32`, `Float64`.\n    - Supported string representations: Integers.\n    - Unsupported string representations (return `default` or `0`): Floating-point numbers (e.g., `'1.23'`), `NaN`, `Inf`, binary, or hexadecimal values.\n- `default` (optional): The `Int32` value to return if the conversion of `expr` is unsuccessful.\n\n{% callout type=\"info\" %}\nIf the input value can't be represented within the bounds of Int32, overflow or underflow of the result occurs.\nThis isn't considered an error.\n{% /callout %}\n\n### Returns\n\nA 32-bit signed integer if successful, otherwise the `default` value (if provided) or `0`. This function will throw an error if the conversion is not possible.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoInt256(expr)\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `expr`: An expression that evaluates to a number or a string representation of a number.\n    - Supported numeric types: `Int8`, `UInt8`, `Int16`, `UInt16`, `Int32`, `UInt32`, `Int64`, `UInt64`, `Int128`, `UInt128`, `Int256`, `UInt256`, `Float32`, `Float64`.\n    - Supported string representations: Integers.\n    - Unsupported string representations: Floating-point numbers (e.g., `'1.23'`), `NaN`, `Inf`, binary, or hexadecimal values.\n\n{% callout type=\"info\" %}\nIf the input value can't be represented within the bounds of Int256, the result over or under flows.\nThis isn't considered an error.\n{% /callout %}\n\n### Returns\n\nA 256-bit signed integer. [Int256](../data-types/int-uint).\n\n{% callout type=\"info\" %}\nThe function uses rounding towards zero, meaning it truncates fractional digits of numbers.\n{% /callout %}\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT\n    toInt256(-256),\n    toInt256(-256.256),\n    toInt256('-256')\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\nRow 1:\n──────\ntoInt256(-256):     -256\ntoInt256(-256.256): -256\ntoInt256('-256'):   -256\n```\n\n## toInt256OrZero\n\nConverts an input value to a 256-bit signed integer, returning `0` if the conversion fails.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoInt256OrZero(x)\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `x`: A string representation of a number.\n    - Supported string representations: Integers.\n    - Unsupported string representations (return `0`): Floating-point numbers (e.g., `'1.23'`), `NaN`, `Inf`, binary, or hexadecimal values.\n\n{% callout type=\"info\" %}\nIf the input value can't be represented within the bounds of Int256, overflow or underflow of the result occurs.\nThis isn't considered an error.\n{% /callout %}\n\n### Returns\n\nA 256-bit signed integer if successful, otherwise `0`. [Int256](../data-types/int-uint).\n\n{% callout type=\"info\" %}\nThe function uses rounding towards zero, meaning it truncates fractional digits of numbers.\n{% /callout %}\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT\n    toInt256OrZero('-256'),\n    toInt256OrZero('abc')\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\nRow 1:\n──────\ntoInt256OrZero('-256'): -256\ntoInt256OrZero('abc'):  0\n```\n\n## toInt256OrNull\n\nConverts an input value to a 256-bit signed integer, returning `NULL` if the conversion fails.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoInt256OrNull(x)\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `x`: A string representation of a number.\n    - Supported string representations: Integers.\n    - Unsupported string representations (return `NULL`): Floating-point numbers (e.g., `'1.23'`), `NaN`, `Inf`, binary, or hexadecimal values.\n\n{% callout type=\"info\" %}\nIf the input value can't be represented within the bounds of Int256, overflow or underflow of the result occurs.\nThis isn't considered an error.\n{% /callout %}\n\n### Returns\n\nA 256-bit signed integer if successful, otherwise `NULL`. [Int256](../data-types/int-uint) / [Nullable](../data-types/nullable).\n\n{% callout type=\"info\" %}\nThe function uses rounding towards zero, meaning it truncates fractional digits of numbers.\n{% /callout %}\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT\n    toInt256OrNull('-256'),\n    toInt256OrNull('abc')\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\nRow 1:\n──────\ntoInt256OrNull('-256'): -256\ntoInt256OrNull('abc'):  ᴺᵁᴸᴸ\n```\n\n## toInt256OrDefault\n\nConverts an input value to a 256-bit signed integer, returning a specified default value if the conversion fails. If no default is provided, it returns `0`.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoInt256OrDefault(expr[, default])\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `expr`: An expression that evaluates to a number or a string representation of a number.\n    - Supported numeric types: `Int8`, `UInt8`, `Int16`, `UInt16`, `Int32`, `UInt32`, `Int64`, `UInt64`, `Int128`, `UInt128`, `Int256`, `UInt256`, `Float32`, `Float64`.\n    - Supported string representations: Integers.\n    - Unsupported string representations (return `default` or `0`): Floating-point numbers (e.g., `'1.23'`), `NaN`, `Inf`, binary, or hexadecimal values.\n- `default` (optional): The `Int256` value to return if the conversion of `expr` is unsuccessful.\n\n{% callout type=\"info\" %}\nIf the input value can't be represented within the bounds of Int256, overflow or underflow of the result occurs.\nThis isn't considered an error.\n{% /callout %}\n\n### Returns\n\nA 256-bit signed integer if successful, otherwise the `default` value (if provided) or `0`. [Int256](../data-types/int-uint).\n\n{% callout type=\"info\" %}\n- The function uses rounding towards zero, meaning it truncates fractional digits of numbers.\n- The default value type should be the same as the cast type.\n{% /callout %}\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT\n    toInt256OrDefault('-256', CAST('-1', 'Int256')),\n    toInt256OrDefault('abc', CAST('-1', 'Int256'))\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\nRow 1:\n──────\ntoInt256OrDefault('-256', CAST('-1', 'Int256')): -256\ntoInt256OrDefault('abc', CAST('-1', 'Int256')):  -1\n```\n\n## toUInt8\n\nConverts an input value to an 8-bit unsigned integer. This function will throw an error if the conversion is not possible.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoUInt8(expr)\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `expr`: An expression that evaluates to a number or a string representation of a number.\n    - Supported numeric types: `Int8`, `UInt8`, `Int16`, `UInt16`, `Int32`, `UInt32`, `Int64`, `UInt64`, `Int128`, `UInt128`, `Int256`, `UInt256`, `Float32`, `Float64`.\n    - Supported string representations: Integers.\n    - Unsupported string representations: Floating-point numbers (e.g., `'1.23'`), `NaN`, `Inf`, binary, or hexadecimal values.\n\n{% callout type=\"info\" %}\nIf the input value can't be represented within the bounds of UInt8, overflow or underflow of the result occurs.\nThis isn't considered an error.\nFor example: `SELECT toUInt8(256) == 0;`.\n{% /callout %}\n\n### Returns\n\nAn 8-bit unsigned integer. [UInt8](../data-types/int-uint).\n\n{% callout type=\"info\" %}\nThe function uses rounding towards zero, meaning it truncates fractional digits of numbers.\n{% /callout %}\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT\n    toUInt8(8),\n    toUInt8(8.8),\n    toUInt8('8')\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\nRow 1:\n──────\ntoUInt8(8):   8\ntoUInt8(8.8): 8\ntoUInt8('8'): 8\n```\n\n## toUInt8OrZero\n\nConverts an input value to an 8-bit unsigned integer, returning `0` if the conversion fails.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoUInt8OrZero(x)\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `x`: A string representation of a number.\n    - Supported string representations: Integers.\n    - Unsupported string representations (return `0`): Floating-point numbers (e.g., `'1.23'`), `NaN`, `Inf`, binary, or hexadecimal values.\n\n{% callout type=\"info\" %}\nIf the input value can't be represented within the bounds of UInt8, overflow or underflow of the result occurs.\nThis isn't considered an error.\n{% /callout %}\n\n### Returns\n\nAn 8-bit unsigned integer if successful, otherwise `0`. [UInt8](../data-types/int-uint).\n\n{% callout type=\"info\" %}\nThe function uses rounding towards zero, meaning it truncates fractional digits of numbers.\n{% /callout %}\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT\n    toUInt8OrZero('-8'),\n    toUInt8OrZero('abc')\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\nRow 1:\n──────\ntoUInt8OrZero('-8'):  0\ntoUInt8OrZero('abc'): 0\n```\n\n## toUInt8OrNull\n\nConverts an input value to an 8-bit unsigned integer, returning `NULL` if the conversion fails.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoUInt8OrNull(x)\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `x`: A string representation of a number.\n    - Supported string representations: Integers.\n    - Unsupported string representations (return `NULL`): Floating-point numbers (e.g., `'1.23'`), `NaN`, `Inf`, binary, or hexadecimal values.\n\n{% callout type=\"info\" %}\nIf the input value can't be represented within the bounds of UInt8, overflow or underflow of the result occurs.\nThis isn't considered an error.\n{% /callout %}\n\n### Returns\n\nAn 8-bit unsigned integer if successful, otherwise `NULL`. [UInt8](../data-types/int-uint) / [Nullable](../data-types/nullable).\n\n{% callout type=\"info\" %}\nThe function uses rounding towards zero, meaning it truncates fractional digits of numbers.\n{% /callout %}\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT\n    toUInt8OrNull('8'),\n    toUInt8OrNull('abc')\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\nRow 1:\n──────\ntoUInt8OrNull('8'):   8\ntoUInt8OrNull('abc'): ᴺᵁᴸᴸ\n```\n\n## toUInt8OrDefault\n\nConverts an input value to an 8-bit unsigned integer, returning a specified default value if the conversion fails. If no default is provided, it returns `0`.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoUInt8OrDefault(expr[, default])\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `expr`: An expression that evaluates to a number or a string representation of a number.\n    - Supported numeric types: `Int8`, `UInt8`, `Int16`, `UInt16`, `Int32`, `UInt32`, `Int64`, `UInt64`, `Int128`, `UInt128`, `Int256`, `UInt256`, `Float32`, `Float64`.\n    - Supported string representations: Integers.\n    - Unsupported string representations (return `default` or `0`): Floating-point numbers (e.g., `'1.23'`), `NaN`, `Inf`, binary, or hexadecimal values.\n- `default` (optional): The `UInt8` value to return if the conversion of `expr` is unsuccessful.\n\n{% callout type=\"info\" %}\nIf the input value can't be represented within the bounds of UInt8, overflow or underflow of the result occurs.\nThis isn't considered an error.\n{% /callout %}\n\n### Returns\n\nAn 8-bit unsigned integer if successful, otherwise the `default` value (if provided) or `0`. [UInt8](../data-types/int-uint).\n\n{% callout type=\"info\" %}\n- The function uses rounding towards zero, meaning it truncates fractional digits of numbers.\n- The default value type should be the same as the cast type.\n{% /callout %}\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT\n    toUInt8OrDefault('8', CAST('0', 'UInt8')),\n    toUInt8OrDefault('abc', CAST('0', 'UInt8'))\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\nRow 1:\n──────\ntoUInt8OrDefault('8', CAST('0', 'UInt8')):   8\ntoUInt8OrDefault('abc', CAST('0', 'UInt8')): 0\n```\n\n## toUInt16\n\nConverts an input value to a 16-bit unsigned integer. This function will throw an error if the conversion is not possible.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoUInt16(expr)\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `expr`: An expression that evaluates to a number or a string representation of a number.\n    - Supported numeric types: `Int8`, `UInt8`, `Int16`, `UInt16`, `Int32`, `UInt32`, `Int64`, `UInt64`, `Int128`, `UInt128`, `Int256`, `UInt256`, `Float32`, `Float64`.\n    - Supported string representations: Integers.\n    - Unsupported string representations: Floating-point numbers (e.g., `'1.23'`), `NaN`, `Inf`, binary, or hexadecimal values.\n\n{% callout type=\"info\" %}\nIf the input value can't be represented within the bounds of UInt16, overflow or underflow of the result occurs.\nThis isn't considered an error.\nFor example: `SELECT toUInt16(65536) == 0;`.\n{% /callout %}\n\n### Returns\n\nA 16-bit unsigned integer. If no default is provided, it returns `0`.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoUInt16OrDefault(expr[, default])\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `expr`: An expression that evaluates to a number or a string representation of a number.\n    - Supported numeric types: `Int8`, `UInt8`, `Int16`, `UInt16`, `Int32`, `UInt32`, `Int64`, `UInt64`, `Int128`, `UInt128`, `Int256`, `UInt256`, `Float32`, `Float64`.\n    - Supported string representations: Integers.\n    - Unsupported string representations (return `default` or `0`): Floating-point numbers (e.g., `'1.23'`), `NaN`, `Inf`, binary, or hexadecimal values.\n- `default` (optional): The `UInt16` value to return if the conversion of `expr` is unsuccessful.\n\n{% callout type=\"info\" %}\nIf the input value can't be represented within the bounds of UInt16, overflow or underflow of the result occurs.\nThis isn't considered an error.\n{% /callout %}\n\n### Returns\n\nA 16-bit unsigned integer if successful, otherwise the `default` value (if provided) or `0`. This function will throw an error if the conversion is not possible.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoUInt32(expr)\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `expr`: An expression that evaluates to a number or a string representation of a number.\n    - Supported numeric types: `Int8`, `UInt8`, `Int16`, `UInt16`, `Int32`, `UInt32`, `Int64`, `UInt64`, `Int128`, `UInt128`, `Int256`, `UInt256`, `Float32`, `Float64`.\n    - Supported string representations: Integers.\n    - Unsupported string representations: Floating-point numbers (e.g., `'1.23'`), `NaN`, `Inf`, binary, or hexadecimal values.\n\n{% callout type=\"info\" %}\nIf the input value can't be represented within the bounds of UInt32, the result over or under flows.\nThis isn't considered an error.\nFor example: `SELECT toUInt32(4294967296) == 0;`\n{% /callout %}\n\n### Returns\n\nA 32-bit unsigned integer. [UInt64](../data-types/int-uint).\n\n{% callout type=\"info\" %}\nThe function uses rounding towards zero, meaning it truncates fractional digits of numbers.\n{% /callout %}\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT\n    toUInt64OrZero('64'),\n    toUInt64OrZero('abc')\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\nRow 1:\n──────\ntoUInt64OrZero('64'):  64\ntoUInt64OrZero('abc'): 0\n```\n\n## toUInt64OrNull\n\nConverts an input value to a 64-bit unsigned integer, returning `NULL` if the conversion fails.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoUInt64OrNull(x)\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `x`: A string representation of a number.\n    - Supported string representations: Integers.\n    - Unsupported string representations (return `NULL`): Floating-point numbers (e.g., `'1.23'`), `NaN`, `Inf`, binary, or hexadecimal values.\n\n{% callout type=\"info\" %}\nIf the input value can't be represented within the bounds of UInt64, overflow or underflow of the result occurs.\nThis isn't considered an error.\n{% /callout %}\n\n### Returns\n\nA 64-bit unsigned integer if successful, otherwise `NULL`. [UInt64](../data-types/int-uint) / [Nullable](../data-types/nullable).\n\n{% callout type=\"info\" %}\nThe function uses rounding towards zero, meaning it truncates fractional digits of numbers.\n{% /callout %}\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT\n    toUInt64OrNull('64'),\n    toUInt64OrNull('abc')\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\nRow 1:\n──────\ntoUInt64OrNull('64'):  64\ntoUInt64OrNull('abc'): ᴺᵁᴸᴸ\n```\n\n## toUInt64OrDefault\n\nConverts an input value to a 64-bit unsigned integer, returning a specified default value if the conversion fails. If no default is provided, it returns `0`.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoUInt64OrDefault(expr[, default])\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `expr`: An expression that evaluates to a number or a string representation of a number.\n    - Supported numeric types: `Int8`, `UInt8`, `Int16`, `UInt16`, `Int32`, `UInt32`, `Int64`, `UInt64`, `Int128`, `UInt128`, `Int256`, `UInt256`, `Float32`, `Float64`.\n    - Supported string representations: Integers.\n    - Unsupported string representations (return `default` or `0`): Floating-point numbers (e.g., `'1.23'`), `NaN`, `Inf`, binary, or hexadecimal values.\n- `default` (optional): The `UInt64` value to return if the conversion of `expr` is unsuccessful.\n\n{% callout type=\"info\" %}\nIf the input value can't be represented within the bounds of UInt64, overflow or underflow of the result occurs.\nThis isn't considered an error.\n{% /callout %}\n\n### Returns\n\nA 64-bit unsigned integer if successful, otherwise the `default` value (if provided) or `0`. [UInt64](../data-types/int-uint).\n\n{% callout type=\"info\" %}\n- The function uses rounding towards zero, meaning it truncates fractional digits of numbers.\n- The default value type should be the same as the cast type.\n{% /callout %}\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT\n    toUInt64OrDefault('64', CAST('0', 'UInt64')),\n    toUInt64OrDefault('abc', CAST('0', 'UInt64'))\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\nRow 1:\n──────\ntoUInt64OrDefault('64', CAST('0', 'UInt64')):  64\ntoUInt64OrDefault('abc', CAST('0', 'UInt64')): 0\n```\n\n## toUInt128\n\nConverts an input value to a 128-bit unsigned integer. This function will throw an error if the conversion is not possible.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoUInt128(expr)\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `expr`: An expression that evaluates to a number or a string representation of a number.\n    - Supported numeric types: `Int8`, `UInt8`, `Int16`, `UInt16`, `Int32`, `UInt32`, `Int64`, `UInt64`, `Int128`, `UInt128`, `Int256`, `UInt256`, `Float32`, `Float64`.\n    - Supported string representations: Integers.\n    - Unsupported string representations: Floating-point numbers (e.g., `'1.23'`), `NaN`, `Inf`, binary, or hexadecimal values.\n\n{% callout type=\"info\" %}\nIf the input value can't be represented within the bounds of UInt128, the result over or under flows.\nThis isn't considered an error.\n{% /callout %}\n\n### Returns\n\nA 128-bit unsigned integer. [UInt128](../data-types/int-uint).\n\n{% callout type=\"info\" %}\nThe function uses rounding towards zero, meaning it truncates fractional digits of numbers.\n{% /callout %}\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT\n    toUInt128(128),\n    toUInt128(128.8),\n    toUInt128('128')\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\nRow 1:\n──────\ntoUInt128(128):   128\ntoUInt128(128.8): 128\ntoUInt128('128'): 128\n```\n\n## toUInt128OrZero\n\nConverts an input value to a 128-bit unsigned integer, returning `0` if the conversion fails.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoUInt128OrZero(expr)\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `expr`: A string representation of a number.\n    - Supported string representations: Integers.\n    - Unsupported string representations (return `0`): Floating-point numbers (e.g., `'1.23'`), `NaN`, `Inf`, binary, or hexadecimal values.\n\n{% callout type=\"info\" %}\nIf the input value can't be represented within the bounds of UInt128, overflow or underflow of the result occurs.\nThis isn't considered an error.\n{% /callout %}\n\n### Returns\n\nA 128-bit unsigned integer if successful, otherwise `0`. [UInt128](../data-types/int-uint).\n\n{% callout type=\"info\" %}\nThe function uses rounding towards zero, meaning it truncates fractional digits of numbers.\n{% /callout %}\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT\n    toUInt128OrZero('128'),\n    toUInt128OrZero('abc')\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\nRow 1:\n──────\ntoUInt128OrZero('128'): 128\ntoUInt128OrZero('abc'): 0\n```\n\n## toUInt128OrNull\n\nConverts an input value to a 128-bit unsigned integer, returning `NULL` if the conversion fails.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoUInt128OrNull(x)\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `x`: A string representation of a number.\n    - Supported string representations: Integers.\n    - Unsupported string representations (return `NULL`): Floating-point numbers (e.g., `'1.23'`), `NaN`, `Inf`, binary, or hexadecimal values.\n\n{% callout type=\"info\" %}\nIf the input value can't be represented within the bounds of UInt128, overflow or underflow of the result occurs.\nThis isn't considered an error.\n{% /callout %}\n\n### Returns\n\nA 128-bit unsigned integer if successful, otherwise `NULL`. [UInt128](../data-types/int-uint) / [Nullable](../data-types/nullable).\n\n{% callout type=\"info\" %}\nThe function uses rounding towards zero, meaning it truncates fractional digits of numbers.\n{% /callout %}\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT\n    toUInt128OrNull('128'),\n    toUInt128OrNull('abc')\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\nRow 1:\n──────\ntoUInt128OrNull('128'): 128\ntoUInt128OrNull('abc'): ᴺᵁᴸᴸ\n```\n\n## toUInt128OrDefault\n\nConverts an input value to a 128-bit unsigned integer, returning a specified default value if the conversion fails. If no default is provided, it returns `0`.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoUInt128OrDefault(expr[, default])\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `expr`: An expression that evaluates to a number or a string representation of a number.\n    - Supported numeric types: `Int8`, `UInt8`, `Int16`, `UInt16`, `Int32`, `UInt32`, `Int64`, `UInt64`, `Int128`, `UInt128`, `Int256`, `UInt256`, `Float32`, `Float64`.\n    - Supported string representations: Integers.\n    - Unsupported string representations (return `default` or `0`): Floating-point numbers (e.g., `'1.23'`), `NaN`, `Inf`, binary, or hexadecimal values.\n- `default` (optional): The `UInt128` value to return if the conversion of `expr` is unsuccessful.\n\n{% callout type=\"info\" %}\nIf the input value can't be represented within the bounds of UInt128, overflow or underflow of the result occurs.\nThis isn't considered an error.\n{% /callout %}\n\n### Returns\n\nA 128-bit unsigned integer if successful, otherwise the `default` value (if provided) or `0`. [UInt128](../data-types/int-uint).\n\n{% callout type=\"info\" %}\n- The function uses rounding towards zero, meaning it truncates fractional digits of numbers.\n- The default value type should be the same as the cast type.\n{% /callout %}\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT\n    toUInt128OrDefault('128', CAST('0', 'UInt128')),\n    toUInt128OrDefault('abc', CAST('0', 'UInt128'))\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\nRow 1:\n──────\ntoUInt128OrDefault('128', CAST('0', 'UInt128')): 128\ntoUInt128OrDefault('abc', CAST('0', 'UInt128')): 0\n```\n\n## toUInt256\n\nConverts an input value to a 256-bit unsigned integer. This function will throw an error if the conversion is not possible.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoUInt256(expr)\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `expr`: An expression that evaluates to a number or a string representation of a number.\n    - Supported numeric types: `Int8`, `UInt8`, `Int16`, `UInt16`, `Int32`, `UInt32`, `Int64`, `UInt64`, `Int128`, `UInt128`, `Int256`, `UInt256`, `Float32`, `Float64`.\n    - Supported string representations: Integers.\n    - Unsupported string representations: Floating-point numbers (e.g., `'1.23'`), `NaN`, `Inf`, binary, or hexadecimal values.\n\n{% callout type=\"info\" %}\nIf the input value can't be represented within the bounds of UInt256, the result over or under flows.\nThis isn't considered an error.\n{% /callout %}\n\n### Returns\n\nA 256-bit unsigned integer. [UInt256](../data-types/int-uint).\n\n{% callout type=\"info\" %}\nThe function uses rounding towards zero, meaning it truncates fractional digits of numbers.\n{% /callout %}\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT\n    toUInt256(256),\n    toUInt256(256.256),\n    toUInt256('256')\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\nRow 1:\n──────\ntoUInt256(256):     256\ntoUInt256(256.256): 256\ntoUInt256('256'):   256\n```\n\n## toUInt256OrZero\n\nConverts an input value to a 256-bit unsigned integer, returning `0` if the conversion fails.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoUInt256OrZero(x)\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `x`: A string representation of a number.\n    - Supported string representations: Integers.\n    - Unsupported string representations (return `0`): Floating-point numbers (e.g., `'1.23'`), `NaN`, `Inf`, binary, or hexadecimal values.\n\n{% callout type=\"info\" %}\nIf the input value can't be represented within the bounds of UInt256, overflow or underflow of the result occurs.\nThis isn't considered an error.\n{% /callout %}\n\n### Returns\n\nA 256-bit unsigned integer if successful, otherwise `0`. [UInt256](../data-types/int-uint).\n\n{% callout type=\"info\" %}\nThe function uses rounding towards zero, meaning it truncates fractional digits of numbers.\n{% /callout %}\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT\n    toUInt256OrZero('256'),\n    toUInt256OrZero('abc')\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\nRow 1:\n──────\ntoUInt256OrZero('256'): 256\ntoUInt256OrZero('abc'): 0\n```\n\n## toUInt256OrNull\n\nConverts an input value to a 256-bit unsigned integer, returning `NULL` if the conversion fails.\n\n### Syntax\n\n```sql\ntoUInt256OrNull(x)\n```\n\n### Arguments\n\n- `x`: A string representation of a number.\n    - Supported string representations: Integers.\n    - Unsupported string representations (return `NULL`): Floating-point numbers (e.g., `'1.23'`), `NaN`, `Inf`, binary, or hexadecimal values.\n\n{% callout type=\"info\" %}\nIf the input value can't be represented within the bounds of UInt256, overflow or underflow of the result occurs.\nThis isn't considered an error.\n{% /callout %}\n\n### Returns\n\nA 256-bit unsigned integer if successful, otherwise `NULL`. [UInt256](../data-types/int-uint) / [Nullable](../data-types/nullable).\n\n{% callout type=\"info\" %}\nThe function uses rounding towards zero, meaning it truncates fractional digits of numbers.\n{% /callout %}\n\n### Example\n\n```sql\nSELECT\n    toUInt256OrNull('256'),\n    toUInt256OrNull('abc')\n```\n\nResult:\n\n```result\nRow 1:\n──────\ntoUInt256OrNull('256'): 256\ntoUInt256OrNull('abc'): ᴺᵁᴸᴸ\n```\n\n## toUInt256OrDefault\n\nConverts an input value to a 256-bit unsigned integer, returning a specified default value if the conversion fails.
